description: Identifies CloudWatch Log Groups with missing, infinite, excessive, or unsafe retention settings. The rule focuses on storage waste while preserving operational, audit, security, and compliance requirements.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: CloudWatch Logs retention changes reduce stored log volume only. They do not reduce ongoing ingestion cost unless log volume, sampling, or verbosity is also changed.

dev.rationale: Short operational debugging window for non-production workloads.

development.rationale: Short operational debugging window for non-production workloads.

test.rationale: Short operational debugging window for non-production workloads.

testing.rationale: Short operational debugging window for non-production workloads.

sandbox.rationale: Ephemeral workloads should not retain logs for long periods.

staging.rationale: Release validation and debugging usually require a longer window than dev/test.

stage.rationale: Release validation and debugging usually require a longer window than dev/test.

preprod.rationale: Pre-production environments often need release-cycle history.

prod.rationale: Production application logs need enough history for incident response and support. Use data classification before reducing below 90 days.

production.rationale: Production application logs need enough history for incident response and support. Use data classification before reducing below 90 days.

audit.rationale: Audit retention must follow organization, legal, and compliance policy. Prefer long-term archival in S3, Glacier, SIEM, or security tooling instead of indefinite CloudWatch Logs storage.

security.rationale: Security retention must follow organization, legal, and compliance policy. Do not reduce based on cost alone.

compliance.rationale: Compliance retention must follow documented retention policy. Do not reduce based on cost alone.

unknown.rationale: Conservative provisional default when owner and environment are not available.

default.rationale: Conservative provisional default when owner and environment are not available.

severity_rules.name: missing_or_infinite_retention
severity_rules.description: Log group has no bounded expiry policy. Logs accumulate indefinitely and create growing storage cost. Apply an environment-appropriate retention_in_days unless audit/security/compliance policy requires a different path.
severity_rules.recommendation: Set a retention period after validating audit and compliance requirements.

severity_rules.name: excessive_retention
severity_rules.condition: retention_in_days > environment_max AND no documented exception
severity_rules.description: Retention exceeds the recommended maximum for the detected environment or data class. Reduce only when operational and compliance requirements are satisfied.

severity_rules.name: silent_log_group_review_candidate
severity_rules.condition: avg(log_bytes_ingested) < zero_ingestion_threshold AND no recent events for delete_candidate_min_silent_days
severity_rules.description: Log group appears silent during the observation period. Confirm owner, upstream service retirement, no subscription/export dependency, and retention obligations before deleting.

severity_rules.name: invalid_or_scenario_only_retention_attribute
severity_rules.condition: aws_cloudwatch_log_group uses retention_days OR other non-provider retention attribute
severity_rules.description: Terraform AWS provider uses retention_in_days. Convert scenario-only or invalid retention attributes to retention_in_days in optimized Terraform.

terraform: Block retention_in_days = 0 unless an approved audit/security/compliance exception is documented.
Require owner, environment, and data_class tags for log groups where possible.
aws: Use EventBridge plus Lambda remediation or approved Terraform modules to set retention on implicitly created log groups.
Export long-term audit or security logs to S3, Glacier, SIEM, or security tooling when CloudWatch Logs is not the cost-effective retention tier.
reporting: Separate storage savings from ingestion optimization.
Report confidence based on whether StoredBytes, cost line items, and environment/data-class evidence are present.
